jokes.text: Why would someone who gets paid to do things be at Greendale?
jokes.setup: Discovery that there's a professional paintball assassin at the community college
jokes.punchline: The implication that Greendale is where people who don't get paid to do things go
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-deprecating humor about the community college setting, economically delivered with character-specific awareness of Greendale's reputation

jokes.text: Who hurt you, Steven? How could any dean be so mean? Oh, you haven't seen how mean this dean can be...en.
jokes.setup: Dramatic confrontation between rival deans with rhyming names
jokes.punchline: The forced extension of 'mean' to 'meanen' to maintain the rhyme scheme
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that fits the dean's theatrical personality, though the forced rhyme is obvious

jokes.text: Wait, did you say 'bean'?
jokes.setup: Previous dramatic declaration ending in forced rhyme
jokes.punchline: Student mishearing the forced 'meanen' as 'bean'
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of the previous dramatic moment, perfectly timed and economical

jokes.text: City College is trying to destroy Greendale, and they're an unstoppable juggle knob. - Juggernaut. - Juggernaut.
jokes.setup: Dramatic exposition about the threat they're facing
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation of 'juggernaut' as 'juggle knob' followed by correction
jokes.explanation: Classic malapropism humor that fits the community college setting of students not quite getting academic vocabulary

jokes.text: In other words, it seems we've left the Western motif and are entering a more of a Star Wars scenario. I know. I wish it happened sooner too.
jokes.setup: Meta-commentary on the show's genre-shifting
jokes.punchline: Admission that Star Wars is preferable to Western themes
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary that's perfectly in character for Abed, acknowledging the show's genre conventions while expressing preference - very craft-forward Community humor

jokes.text: Okay, she is just saying that to fit in.
jokes.setup: Previous harsh dismissal of Britta
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the dismissal was just peer pressure, not genuine criticism
jokes.explanation: Decent follow-up that deflates the previous moment but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: First off, pop, pop! And second, Magnitude's a one-man party. A one-man party can't be in an alliance. That's a paradox.
jokes.setup: Magnitude addressing the group formally
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase followed by absurd logical reasoning about party/alliance incompatibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Magnitude character moment - his catchphrase plus absurd but internally logical reasoning about his party status

jokes.text: I agree to disagree. - Easy, paradox.
jokes.setup: Response to Magnitude's paradox statement
jokes.punchline: Using 'paradox' as a nickname/insult like calling someone 'troublemaker'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay using 'paradox' as an address, good economy but not deeply funny

jokes.text: Their balls? - No. - Just below their balls? - Their wallet, Leonard. - I think it's called a taint.
jokes.setup: 'Hit them where it hurts' metaphor
jokes.punchline: Anatomical literalism escalating to 'taint' identification
jokes.explanation: Standard escalating crude humor - gets laughs but relies on anatomical references rather than wit

jokes.text: Whatever, pal. I ain't in it for your revolution. I'm in it for me.
jokes.setup: Jeff's inspirational speech about unity
jokes.punchline: Abed adopting Han Solo's selfish persona
jokes.explanation: Great character moment where Abed commits to role-playing Han Solo, perfectly executed with the right dialogue style

jokes.text: I know, but I'm calling dibs on the Han Solo role before Jeff slouches into it by default.
jokes.setup: Confusion about why Abed is acting differently
jokes.punchline: Meta-awareness that Jeff would naturally fall into the Han Solo archetype
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary where Abed recognizes narrative archetypes and Jeff's natural fit for Han Solo - quintessential Community humor

jokes.text: Give me the vest, Laser Breath, before our conversation gets nasty.
jokes.setup: Abed needing Star-Burns' vest for his Han Solo costume
jokes.punchline: Using 'Laser Breath' as an insult (Han Solo reference) with mock threat
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency as Abed fully commits to Han Solo persona with appropriate insults

jokes.text: Damn it, Shirley. Forget your newborn child and think about the people that need you.
jokes.setup: Shirley mentioning she needs to get home to her baby
jokes.punchline: Jeff's callous prioritization of paintball over motherhood
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy showing Jeff's priorities completely warped by competition - perfectly in character and quotable

jokes.text: I don't step up to being leader, Troy. I reluctantly accept it when it's thrust upon me.
jokes.setup: Troy challenging Jeff's assumption of leadership
jokes.punchline: Jeff's grandiose description of his completely voluntary leadership grab
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff character moment - his narcissistic reframing of obvious power-grabbing as reluctant duty

jokes.text: Pop... P... Pop what? Pop what? What is he trying to say? Pop what, Magnitude?
jokes.setup: Magnitude apparently being shot/dying
jokes.punchline: His inability to complete his signature catchphrase in his final moments
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-based comedy - Magnitude's 'death' scene focused on his inability to complete his defining catchphrase, perfect callback structure

jokes.text: All my men have been registered at Greendale for weeks.
jokes.setup: Accusation that City College students can't play in Greendale game
jokes.punchline: The bureaucratic loophole of temporary registration for paintball
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor about bureaucratic technicalities but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Those guys are ballers, yo. I hope you like getting balled.
jokes.setup: Praising Jeff's study group
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to make 'baller' into a threat
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that doesn't quite work - the pun feels forced and the threat is nonsensical

jokes.text: You'd certainly be doing Jeff Winger a big favor.
jokes.setup: City College students about to shoot Pierce
jokes.punchline: Pierce suggesting his death would benefit Jeff
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce character moment showing his awareness of being disliked, delivered with dark humor

jokes.text: 'For Greendale' on three. One, two... Jeff, every second counts. 'For Greendale' on two. One... Guys. For Greendale!
jokes.setup: Attempting to do a team cheer
jokes.punchline: The countdown getting increasingly rushed until they just give up on coordination
jokes.explanation: Nice escalating urgency gag that shows the group's inability to coordinate even simple tasks under pressure

jokes.text: Our sperm counts are higher. Even in our women.
jokes.setup: Listing City College's superiority
jokes.punchline: Bizarre biological claim about female sperm counts
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation that makes no biological sense but is perfectly delivered - very quotable weird humor

jokes.text: Honestly, ask yourself, what does 'liberal arts' even mean?
jokes.setup: Continuing his dismissal of Greendale
jokes.punchline: Questioning the fundamental meaning of liberal arts education
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-commentary on community college education that hits close to home for the show's premise

jokes.text: Welcome to Greendale. - You're already dead.
jokes.setup: Riffing on Greendale's motto
jokes.punchline: Dark twist changing 'accepted' to 'dead'
jokes.explanation: Effective dark twist on the motto callback, economically delivered with good structure

jokes.text: Sure thing, your worship.
jokes.setup: Annie asking for help moving equipment
jokes.punchline: Abed's Han Solo persona responding with Princess Leia's sarcastic address
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with Abed maintaining Han Solo role-play with appropriate Star Wars quotes

jokes.text: Stop trying to be Han Solo. It's making me embarrassed for you.
jokes.setup: Abed's continued Han Solo act
jokes.punchline: Annie's secondhand embarrassment at the role-playing
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe humor with Annie being the straight-man to Abed's committed performance

jokes.text: I hope I don't get shot waiting out here. I'd hate to go home to my babies.
jokes.setup: Shirley being assigned lookout duty
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic reversal where going home to her children would be the bad outcome
jokes.explanation: Great Shirley moment showing her complete investment in the game over maternal duties, perfect ironic delivery

jokes.text: Paint in sprinklers? Are we the little rascals? - I was.
jokes.setup: Jeff mocking Troy's plan as childish
jokes.punchline: Troy's matter-of-fact admission of being in The Little Rascals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery that completely deflates Jeff's insult while adding unexpected character background - perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: I don't take orders from girls. Because they don't talk to me.
jokes.setup: Star-Burns appearing to make a sexist statement
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's actually about his social isolation
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that starts as offensive and becomes pathetic - excellent structure and character insight with great economy

jokes.text: Watch it, Quasar face. Annie's a good kid and a better shot. So listen up, or you'll be floating home. Thank you, Abed. Sorry. Han.
jokes.setup: Abed defending Annie from Star-Burns
jokes.punchline: Annie thanking him by name then correcting herself to his role-play character
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Annie's growing acceptance of Abed's role-play commitment

jokes.text: Now we have the tactical advantage of knowing that Jeff Winger wants to be a ballerina, and that his Achilles heel is...wieners.
jokes.setup: Pierce having been captured and interrogated
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous 'intelligence' he's sharing about Jeff
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce humor - completely unhelpful 'intelligence' that's more embarrassing for him than useful to enemies

jokes.text: What took them so long?
jokes.setup: Learning Pierce was kicked out of study group after two years
jokes.punchline: Suggesting two years was too long to put up with Pierce
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that reinforces Pierce's unlikability from an outsider perspective

jokes.text: For a no-good, laser-faced, jabba scoundrel. You're good, kid. Don't get cocky.
jokes.setup: Annie complimenting Abed despite his role-playing
jokes.punchline: Mix of insults and Star Wars quotes maintaining the Han/Leia dynamic
jokes.explanation: Nice continuation of the Star Wars role-play with Annie joining in the dynamic

jokes.text: Operation 'Troy's Awesome Plan' is living up to its name.
jokes.setup: Troy's plan working as intended
jokes.punchline: His immodest naming of his own plan
jokes.explanation: Good Troy character moment showing his pride and tendency toward grandiose naming

jokes.text: Squad 'B' commencing operation 'Actual Operation.'
jokes.setup: Jeff coordinating his part of the plan
jokes.punchline: His more serious, less creative operation name
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast to Troy's dramatic naming, showing Jeff's more straightforward approach

jokes.text: Some of us won't make it. But there is a place where we will all see each other again, and that place is Denny's. Which Denny's? We'll figure it out later, Leonard. The one near the 15 exit? I'm banned from there. Well, then I guess I'll see you in hell.
jokes.setup: Jeff's dramatic pre-battle speech
jokes.punchline: The inspirational afterlife becomes a debate about which Denny's location, ending with Leonard being banned
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity that deflates heroic speech with mundane logistics, perfectly capped with Leonard's ban

jokes.text: Then let's kick some taint.
jokes.setup: Jeff giving final rallying cry before battle
jokes.punchline: Using Leonard's earlier crude anatomical term as battle cry
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Jeff incorporating Leonard's crude vocabulary into his leadership style. The incongruity makes it funny.

jokes.text: Can we move this along? I'm missing CSI.
jokes.setup: In the middle of intense paintball strategy session
jokes.punchline: Vicki prioritizing her TV viewing over the 'war'
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific deflation showing how not everyone is equally invested in the paintball drama

jokes.text: I may be stuck in this vent. It is too early to tell.
jokes.setup: Garrett attempting to evacuate through air vent
jokes.punchline: His uncertain and overly measured assessment of being stuck
jokes.explanation: Perfect Garrett line with his characteristic uncertain delivery about a simple physical situation

jokes.text: Commence operation 'Troy's Awesome Leadership Is Never in Doubt' plan.
jokes.setup: Troy taking command of the backup plan
jokes.punchline: His even more grandiose and self-aggrandizing operation name
jokes.explanation: Escalation of Troy's grandiose naming tendency, showing his growing confidence and ego

jokes.text: Hey, Quendra with a Q-U, they can kill most of us, but as long as nobody gives up, somebody will make it through. Understand?
jokes.setup: Jeff giving inspirational speech to student
jokes.punchline: Addressing someone by their unusual name spelling in the middle of a dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist detail that deflates the heroic moment with pedantic name pronunciation

jokes.text: Well, I'm out. We lost. See you at Denny's? Denny's is for winners.
jokes.setup: Student getting eliminated and referencing earlier Denny's meetup plan
jokes.punchline: Jeff's harsh exclusion of losers from Denny's
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Jeff's competitive nature overriding his earlier inspirational inclusivity

jokes.text: Leonard, we're losing. Britta, I've been in a few real wars, but this one is actually the most terrifying.
jokes.setup: Leonard's military background being established
jokes.punchline: Paintball being worse than actual warfare
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation that gives Leonard character depth while maintaining the show's commitment to paintball intensity

jokes.text: I had a dream it would end this way.
jokes.setup: Abed in his final moments of the paintball game
jokes.punchline: His prophetic acceptance, staying in Han Solo character
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Abed maintaining his role-play commitment even in defeat

jokes.text: Will you still be Han Solo after we die? 'Fraid not, doll. Once I'm gone, I'm gone.
jokes.setup: Annie asking about Abed's character commitment beyond death
jokes.punchline: Abed's matter-of-fact explanation of role-play limitations
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-moment about the boundaries of character role-play with typical Abed logic

jokes.text: It's my h-heart. Hey, buddy, you okay?
jokes.setup: City College student apparently having a heart attack
jokes.punchline: Casual inquiry about someone's cardiac emergency
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup but the casual response adds some humor

jokes.text: Eat paint, you storm-troopin', City College knob gobblers.
jokes.setup: Shirley making her heroic entrance
jokes.punchline: Sweet Shirley using aggressive language and crude insults
jokes.explanation: Perfect character subversion with religious Shirley using the most aggressive language in the episode - excellent character integration

jokes.text: Your mother's lover. What? What? I win!
jokes.setup: Mystery about who the final player is
jokes.punchline: Pierce's crude revelation followed by victory declaration
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce moment with characteristic inappropriate revelation and triumph

jokes.text: And you can make the check out... to Greendale.
jokes.setup: Pierce appearing to claim the prize money for himself
jokes.punchline: Surprising donation to the school after dramatic pause
jokes.explanation: Good character redemption moment with effective misdirection and dramatic timing

jokes.text: Too risky. Sequels are almost always disappointing.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to take Anthropology 201 (sequel to their first anthro class)
jokes.punchline: Abed's meta-commentary on sequel quality applied to course selection
jokes.explanation: Peak Community meta-humor. Abed applying film theory to mundane course selection while subtly commenting on the show's own sequel episodes. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Since when are you in favor of playing it safe, Han? Cool down, Annie. I was only Han Solo because the context demanded it.
jokes.setup: Annie calling Abed 'Han' in regular conversation
jokes.punchline: Abed's analytical explanation of his character choice as contextual necessity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-analysis of his own character choices, showing his analytical approach to social dynamics

jokes.text: Yeah, he uses some kind of crystal instead of deodorant.
jokes.setup: Returning Star-Burns' vest
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about Star-Burns' hygiene choices
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Star-Burns that fits his alternative lifestyle persona

jokes.text: Just came back for my day planner. Pretend I'm not even here. You guys are great at that.
jokes.setup: Pierce interrupting the group meeting
jokes.punchline: His bitter observation about being ignored
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce bitterness with self-awareness about his exclusion, delivered with characteristic passive-aggression

jokes.text: Come on, Pierce. We all know this is no 'day planner.'
jokes.setup: Pierce claiming he came back for his day planner
jokes.punchline: Jeff calling out that it's obviously not actually a day planner
jokes.explanation: Setup for Pierce's transparent excuse. Jeff's air quotes around 'day planner' shows everyone knows Pierce is making up reasons to return.

jokes.text: it appears I'm running late to drink a bottle of wine in my bathing suit.
jokes.setup: Pierce reading from his day planner
jokes.punchline: His bizarre evening activity scheduled like a normal appointment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Pierce moment combining loneliness, eccentricity, and precise scheduling - perfectly absurd and quotable

jokes.text: Probably for the same reason I've been married seven times. I guess I assume eventually I'll be rejected, so I, you know, test people, push them, until they prove me right.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining his pattern of short friendships
jokes.punchline: Self-aware analysis of his self-sabotaging behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent character development moment showing Pierce's rare self-awareness about his toxic patterns - very well written

jokes.text: And now he'll come slinking back in five, four, three, two...
jokes.setup: Annie predicting Pierce will return immediately
jokes.punchline: The countdown reaching zero with no Pierce return
jokes.explanation: Effective misdirection with good structure, though the punchline is silence rather than dialogue

jokes.text: Actually, we kind of did that. Haven't you been to the library? - I'm working my way there.
jokes.setup: Jeff suggesting paint hose idea not knowing it already happened
jokes.punchline: Jeff admitting he hasn't seen the aftermath of their own plan
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the paint sprinkler plan with Jeff's typical avoidance of consequences

jokes.text: Cool, cool, cool. Slippery.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation ending
jokes.punchline: Abed's signature verbal tic followed by unexpected 'slippery' comment
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed verbal pattern with the unexpected addition of 'slippery' - good character consistency
